items.summary: Small business owners are urging Labor to rethink more than just the eligibility thresholds for capital gains tax concessions.

items.title: How to grow garlic in a small garden

items.title: Johnny Galvatron left his band to make video games. Now, Mixtape explores 90s nostalgia through music
items.summary: Melbourne's Johnny Galvatron gave up the rockstar life to become a BAFTA-winning game developer. Now, his studio's latest release, Mixtape, re-imagines the music game as an eclectic playlist of genres and mechanics.

items.title: ABC news quiz: A drone drama, a 'dream job' and an old insult making a comeback

items.title: Trump says Murdoch indicated he would 'handle' newspaper's Epstein story

items.title: Iran fires missile at US air base in Kuwait in retaliation for American attack
items.summary: US President Donald Trump says he is not yet satisfied by negotiations to end the war in Iran, and the US is not discussing easing sanctions, which is one of Tehran's key demands.

items.title: Man to face court over alleged fuel drive-offs using trucks in Adelaide's north

items.title: Former police officer says he got into bed with woman but denies raping her, court hears
items.summary: A former South Australian police officer says he got into bed with a woman while she was sleeping and caressed her thigh soon after she rejected his advances, but denies raping her, a court has heard.

items.title: Coronial inquest probes NSW Police staffing levels and mental health support
items.summary: An inquest into the death of a NSW Police recruit who was less than three months into the job hears differing opinions about whether it was related to his work.

items.title: Hawks to go to AFL over Watson's disallowed goal

items.title: Inquest finds RAAF serviceman Gary Sargeant's death was preventable

items.title: Victoria's political donations cap to double under proposed Electoral Act changes

items.title: Hudson Young finds method in the madness for long-awaited State of Origin arrival

items.title: Ali Khamenei's Iranian home a site of mourning three months after his death
items.summary: Outside the Tehran compound that was home to now-dead Iranian supreme leader Ali Khamenei, hundreds of his supporters gather every night to mourn the only leader many of them have ever known.

items.title: US Justice Department launches criminal probe into E Jean Carroll, source says
items.summary: The probe is focused on whether Carroll committed perjury in testimony tied to two civil lawsuits that she won against Mr Trump — one tied to ‌her allegations that he sexually abused her in a New York department store.

items.title: Perth mother who lost two children to rare genetic condition wants mandatory tests

items.title: Hamas confirms that Israeli air strike killed its new military leader in Gaza

items.title: French Open handshake drama spices up women's draw as Elena Rybakina eliminated
items.summary: There is no love lost between Tamara Korpatsch and Wang Xinyu after a testy match as Elena Rybakina's hopes of another grand slam title go up in smoke.

items.title: Not All Clubs calls for a stand against gender-based violence with Wear Red round
items.summary: Not All Clubs calls out inequality in sporting organisations across the country. This weekend, the group is calling on teams to wear red armbands in memory of the women whose lives have been claimed by gender-based violence this year.

items.summary: Queensland's coach bats away questions about the notable absence of Brisbane's star fullback after seeing his side cough up a 20-point lead.

items.summary: Modelling predicts that the algal bloom that has plagued South Australia's coastlines since March 2025 will be moving further through the Spencer Gulf in the next few months.

items.title: Rainy weather drenching Australia's east could turn May into one of the wettest on record
items.summary: A series of low-pressure systems are drenching Australia this week, quickly turning this May into one of the wettest on record for parts of the country's east.

items.title: Stolen Generations brothers share their story ahead of Reconciliation Week
items.summary: Iain Seymour never knew he was Aboriginal until finding out he was adopted three years ago and then meeting his brother Allen Edwards, the son of a woman who played a key part in setting up a poignant memorial to the long-term effects of the Stolen Generations. Warning: This story contains images of Indigenous people who have died.

items.title: Load limits on Victorian bridges 'wreaking havoc' as councils stuggle to fund maintenance
items.summary: Councils across regional Victoria say they are being forced to ban trucks from some bridges because they cannot afford to fix them to keep them safe.

items.title: Can farmers become less reliant on synthetic fertiliser? Some are trying

items.title: Matthew Perry's assistant jailed for role in Friends actor's death
items.summary: Matthew Perry's live-in personal assistant, who had a central role in the Friends star's descent into ketamine addiction and injected him with the fatal dose of the drug, has been sentenced to three years and five months in prison.
